title: CandidateProfile
description: Workspace-private candidate profile for fit analysis. Created manually — no resume upload required. profile_version is injected at creation time so that cache keys derived from profile_hash naturally invalidate when the schema interpretation changes.

profile_version.description: FIT_PROFILE_VERSION at creation time. Injected by profile_service.create_manual_profile(). Because profile_hash = md5(full profile JSON), bumping this version changes the hash and automatically invalidates cached Fit Reports.

display_name.description: Optional label for this profile version, e.g. 'Senior Associate 2026'.

years_experience.description: Total years of relevant professional experience.

current_background.description: 2-3 sentence summary of current role and primary focus area.

domain_experience.description: Finance/risk domains with hands-on experience. e.g. ['Market Risk', 'Valuation Control', 'Credit Analytics']

technical_skills.description: Programming languages, data tools, risk systems. e.g. ['Python', 'SQL', 'Bloomberg', 'Murex']

analytical_methods.description: Quantitative methods and frameworks. e.g. ['VaR', 'Greeks', 'Scenario Analysis', 'Model Validation']

finance_domains.description: Asset classes or product types. e.g. ['Equities', 'Fixed Income', 'Derivatives', 'Structured Products']

representative_projects.description: Most important work evidence. Each item should be concrete enough for LLM evidence-based matching. Structured rather than free text so match_service can cite specific projects in strong_matches.

title.description: Short label for this project. Optional.

description.description: What you did and the business context. Be specific.

skills_used.description: Skills and tools directly exercised in this project.

quantified_impact.description: Optional metric or outcome. e.g. 'Reduced daily reconciliation time by 40%.' Best to include when available.

target_workstreams.description: Workstream IDs from workstream_taxonomy.yaml the candidate is targeting. UI/filter use.

target_roles.description: Role titles or role-type keywords the candidate is open to. UI/filter use.

constraints.description: Location, seniority, compensation, or other preferences. UI/filter use only — not fed to LLM analysis.
